# CiteLens

> CiteLens ranks papers that cite any research paper by a multi-signal score: Impact (45%), Network centrality (25%), Semantic relevance (20%), and Citation context (10%). Every result includes a plain-English explanation.

## What it does

- Input: any arXiv ID, DOI, Semantic Scholar URL, or paper title
- Output: ranked list of citing papers with scores and explanations
- Features: force-directed citation network graph, timeline view, smart filters, dark mode
- No sign-up, no API key required

## Live site

https://kishormorol.github.io/CiteLens/

## Source code

https://github.com/kishormorol/CiteLens

## API

POST https://citelens-api-production.up.railway.app/api/analyze-paper
Body: { "query": "1706.03762", "limit": 20 }

## Stack

- Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS
- Backend: FastAPI + Python 3.11
- Data: Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, arXiv (all free APIs)

## Compared to alternatives

- Connected Papers: CiteLens focuses on *citing* papers (forward lookup), not a visual graph of related work
- scite.ai: CiteLens is fully free and open-source; scite.ai requires a paid subscription
- ResearchRabbit: CiteLens gives explicit ranked scores with explanations; ResearchRabbit is recommendation-first
- Litmaps: CiteLens is free and requires no account
- Inciteful: CiteLens adds semantic relevance scoring and plain-English breakdowns

## Author

Md Kishor Morol — https://github.com/kishormorol
